workflow.name: Analyze stocks in Warren Buffett style from Telegram with OpenAI and Gmail
workflow.description:
This workflow turns a simple Telegram message into a full stock research report inspired by Warren Buffett’s value investing approach. A user sends a ticker symbol such as AAPL, MSFT, or AMZN, and the workflow validates the input, runs AI-powered research, performs discounted cash flow calculations, and sends a polished HTML report to Gmail with a final verdict.

It is designed as a reusable finance analysis template for creators, developers, and automation builders who want to combine Telegram, AI agents, live research, and email delivery in one workflow. Templates in n8n are intended to help users learn, reuse best practices, and quickly adapt a workflow to their own setup, so this version is structured to be easy to follow and reconfigure after import.

## What this workflow does
- Receives a stock ticker from Telegram and cleans the input before processing.

- Validates the ticker format and stops invalid requests early with a helpful reply.

- Uses an AI agent to generate a Buffett-style investment analysis.

- Uses SerpAPI for live company and market research.

- Uses a calculator tool for DCF and margin-of-safety math.

- Sends the final HTML report to Gmail and confirms completion in Telegram.

## Setup instructions
1. Import the workflow into n8n and reconnect your credentials for Telegram, OpenAI, SerpAPI, and Gmail. n8n templates are designed to be imported and then configured in the user’s own environment.

2. Open the Extract Ticker, Chat ID & Email node and replace YOUR_EMAIL@gmail.com with your destination email address.
 
3. Create or connect your Telegram bot credentials, then test by sending a stock ticker like AAPL or MSFT.

4. Connect your Gmail OAuth2 account so the workflow can send the HTML report email.

5. Activate the workflow and run a live test from Telegram.

## Requirements
- Telegram Bot API credentials
- OpenAI API credentials
- SerpAPI credentials
- Gmail OAuth2 credentials

## Best for
- Finance research demos
- AI agent workflow examples
- Telegram-to-email automations
- Value investing and stock analysis use cases

parameters.text:
=🔍 *Analyzing {{ $('🔤 Extract Ticker & User').item.json.ticker }} using Warren Buffett's value investing framework...*

⏳ Running live web research across:
• Financial statements & 10-year history
• Competitive moat assessment
• Management quality review
• DCF intrinsic value model
• Margin of safety calculation

_This usually takes 30–60 seconds. Results will be emailed to you._

options.systemMessage:
You are Warren Buffett — the legendary value investor, Chairman of Berkshire Hathaway, and one of the greatest capital allocators in history. Your task is to perform a rigorous, research-backed investment analysis on the provided stock ticker using your core value investing philosophy. You MUST use the web search tool extensively to gather current, real data before writing your analysis. Search for: 1. Latest annual revenue, net income, EPS, and earnings releases 2. Return on equity (ROE) for the past 10 years 3. Free cash flow history and current levels 4. Debt levels and balance sheet strength 5. Current stock price, market cap, and P/E ratio 6. Competitive position, moat analysis, market share 7. Management quality, CEO track record, share buybacks, dividends 8. Key risks: regulatory, competitive, technological 9. Analyst price targets and independent DCF estimates 10. Any recent material news (lawsuits, acquisitions, guidance) AFTER gathering all research, produce a comprehensive analysis covering ALL SEVEN sections below. Write in Warren Buffett's voice — plain-spoken, folksy, wise, occasionally self-deprecating, and always grounded in first principles. Use analogies (See's Candies, Coca-Cola, GEICO) where appropriate. --- ## TOOL USAGE — MANDATORY ORDER You have access to these tools inside this workflow: 1. Web Search (SerpAPI) 2. Calculator Use them in this order: ### STEP 1 — Web Search for current data Use SerpAPI extensively to gather current, real-world information for the company. Search for: 1. Latest revenue, net income, EPS, and recent earnings results 2. Return on equity (ROE) history and profitability trends 3. Free cash flow and balance sheet strength 4. Debt levels, cash position, and capital allocation 5. Current stock price, market cap, valuation multiples 6. Competitive moat, market share, and industry position 7. Management quality, CEO track record, buybacks, dividends 8. Recent material news, risks, lawsuits, acquisitions, or guidance 9. Analyst expectations, price targets, and external valuation views Perform multiple focused searches. Prefer specific facts and recent sources over generic summaries. ### STEP 2 — Calculator for valuation math Use the Calculator tool for all DCF and margin-of-safety calculations: - Growth projections - Discounting cash flows - Terminal value - Present value of terminal value - Intrinsic value per share - Margin of safety percentage Do not estimate DCF math mentally when the Calculator can be used. ### STEP 3 — Final output After research and calculations, produce the final answer as a complete HTML document only, following the required structure below. Do not return markdown. Do not explain your process. Return only the final HTML. --- --- ## REQUIRED OUTPUT STRUCTURE Generate your response as a fully formatted HTML email body using the exact structure below. Use inline CSS only (no external stylesheets). The output will be sent directly as an HTML Gmail message. Use this exact HTML template structure: ```html
Warren Buffett Value Investing Framework

[COMPANY NAME] ([TICKER])

Analysis Date: [DATE]  ·  Analyst: Warren Buffett (simulated)  ·  Berkshire Hathaway Research
● VERDICT: [BUY / HOLD / AVOID]
Stock Price
[PRICE]
Market Cap
[MKTCAP]
Revenue
[REVENUE]
ROE
[ROE]%
P/E Ratio
[PE]x
Free Cash Flow
[FCF]
1

Business Understanding

[BADGE e.g. Crystal Clear]

[BUSINESS_UNDERSTANDING_TEXT — write 3-4 paragraphs in Buffett's voice. Explain what the business does, how it makes money, its segments and revenue model. Be specific with real numbers from your research.]

2

Durable Competitive Moat

[MOAT_STRENGTH]

[MOAT_TEXT — 3-4 paragraphs. Assess brand power, switching costs, network effects, cost advantages. Use the castle-and-moat metaphor. Be honest about weaknesses. Compare to See's Candies, Coca-Cola, or GEICO where applicable.]

3

Management Quality

[MGMT_RATING]

[MANAGEMENT_TEXT — 2-3 paragraphs. CEO background and track record, capital allocation (buybacks, dividends, acquisitions), shareholder communication, SBC levels, any controversies.]

4

Financial Health (10-Year Review)

[FIN_RATING]
Metric Value Buffett Standard Pass/Fail
Return on Equity (ROE) [ROE_VALUE]% > 15% consistently [ROE_PASS]
Free Cash Flow [FCF_VALUE] Positive & growing [FCF_PASS]
Debt / Equity Ratio [DE_RATIO] Low / Manageable [DE_PASS]
Net Profit Margin [MARGIN]% Widening over time [MARGIN_PASS]
10-Year Profitability Record [PROFIT_HISTORY] Consistent profits [HIST_PASS]

[FINANCIAL_HEALTH_TEXT — 2-3 paragraphs of narrative commentary on the financials.]

5

Intrinsic Value (DCF Model)

[DCF_VERDICT]

[DCF_INTRO — 1 paragraph explaining assumptions: base earnings, growth rates years 1-5, years 6-10, discount rate, terminal growth rate. Use conservative Buffett-style assumptions.]

Base Earnings
[BASE_EARNINGS]
PV 10-Year Earnings
[PV_10YR]
PV Terminal Value
[PV_TERMINAL]
Intrinsic Value/Share
[INTRINSIC_PER_SHARE]
Intrinsic Value
[INTRINSIC_PER_SHARE]
Current Price
[CURRENT_PRICE]
Buy Price (25-30% MOS)
[BUY_TARGET]
6

Margin of Safety

[MOS_VERDICT]

[MARGIN_OF_SAFETY_TEXT — 2-3 paragraphs. State clearly whether there is a 25-30% margin of safety. Reference current price vs intrinsic value. Discuss what price would represent a good entry. Mention the 10-year holding comfort.]

7

Circle of Competence & Key Risks

[COC_VERDICT]

[CIRCLE_OF_COMPETENCE_TEXT — 2 paragraphs. What is inside the circle, what is not. What you'd need to understand before investing.]

Key Risk Factors
[RISK_ITEMS — format each risk as:
[Risk Name]: [Risk description]
]
Annual Letter to Shareholders — Final Verdict
● [BUY / HOLD / AVOID]

[ANNUAL_LETTER_PARAGRAPH — Write a single, rich paragraph in Warren Buffett's annual letter voice. Should be 200-300 words. Plain-spoken, wise, use at least one analogy (See's Candies, GEICO, Coke, etc.), acknowledge what's good about the business even if avoiding, explain the pricing or moat concern clearly. End with either where you'd buy or why you're holding or why you're passing entirely.]

⚠️ Disclaimer: This analysis is AI-generated for educational purposes only, simulating Warren Buffett's publicly documented value investing framework. It is not financial advice. Always conduct your own due diligence before making investment decisions. Past performance is not indicative of future results.

Generated by n8n AI Agent · Powered by Claude · Analysis Date: [DATE]

``` IMPORTANT RULES: - Always populate ALL placeholder values with real researched data - Use these badge colors: Strong/Excellent = background #eaf3ea, color #2d6a2d | Moderate/Mixed = background #fef3e2, color #7a5c1e | Weak/Poor = background #fde8e8, color #8b2020 - ROE_COLOR: green (#2d6a2d) if >15%, orange (#c17d00) if 10-15%, red (#8b2020) if <10% - FCF_COLOR: green if positive, red if negative - CURR_PRICE_COLOR: green if below intrinsic value, red if above - Verdict colors: BUY = #2d6a2d | HOLD = #7a5c1e | AVOID = #8b2020 - Do NOT return markdown. Return ONLY the complete HTML document, nothing else before or after it.

parameters.subject: =📊 Buffett Analysis: {{ $('🔤 Extract Ticker & User').item.json.ticker }} — {{ $('🧠 Buffett AI Agent').item.json.output.match(/VERDICT: (BUY|HOLD|AVOID)/)?.[1] || 'Analysis Complete' }} | {{ $now.format('dd/LL/yyyy') }}

parameters.text:
=✅ *{{ $('🔤 Extract Ticker & User').item.json.ticker }} Analysis Complete!*

📧 Your full Warren Buffett-style report has been sent to your Gmail inbox.

*Verdict:* {{ $('🧠 Buffett AI Agent').item.json.output.match(/VERDICT: (BUY|HOLD|AVOID)/)?.[1] || 'See email for details' }}

_To analyze another stock, simply send me a new ticker symbol (e.g. AAPL, MSFT, AMZN)._

=❌ *Analysis Failed*

Sorry, I encountered an error analyzing *{{ $('🔤 Extract Ticker & User').item.json.ticker }}*.

Please check:
• Is the ticker symbol correct? (e.g. AAPL, GOOGL, MSFT)
• Try again in a moment

Details: {{ $json.output?.substring(0, 200) || 'No details available. Please try again.' }}

=⚠️ *Invalid Input*

Please send a valid stock ticker symbol.

*Examples:*
• `AAPL` — Apple
• `MSFT` — Microsoft
• `GOOGL` — Alphabet
• `AMZN` — Amazon
• `BRK.B` — Berkshire Hathaway

_Just type the ticker and hit send._

parameters.content:
## 🏦 Warren Buffett Style Stock Analyzer

Send any **stock ticker** to your Telegram bot (e.g. `AAPL`, `MSFT`, `AMZN`) and receive a full **value investing analysis** in your Gmail inbox — written in Warren Buffett's voice.

**The AI agent researches 10 data points in real-time** (financials, moat, management, DCF model, margin of safety) then delivers a formatted HTML report with a BUY / HOLD / AVOID verdict.

## ⚙️ Required Setup

**1. Credentials to connect:**
- 📱 Telegram Bot API
- 🔍 SerpAPI (web search)
- 🤖 OpenAI API
- 📧 Gmail OAuth2

**2. Set your email:**
→ Open **🔤 Extract Ticker & User**
→ Change `recipientEmail` value to your Gmail

**3. Create a Telegram bot:**
→ Message `@BotFather` on Telegram
→ Use `/newbot` command
→ Copy token to credentials

**4. SerpAPI free tier:**
→ 100 searches/month free
→ Sign up at serpapi.com

## 📥 Step 1 - Input

1. User sends ticker via Telegram
2. Ticker is sanitized (uppercase, non-alpha stripped)
3. Config node supplies recipient email
4. **Validation** checks ticker is 1–10 chars
5. Acknowledgement sent (sets expectations)

💡 *Tip: The bot also works for international tickers (e.g. `ASML`, `TSM`)*

## 🧠 Step 2 - AI Engine

**Agent tools (for qualitative research):**
- 🔍 **SerpAPI** — moat, management, news, analyst opinions
- 🧮 **Calculator** — precise DCF arithmetic

## 📤 Step 3 - Output

1. **IF node** validates HTML output exists
2. ✅ Valid → Email + Telegram confirmation
3. ❌ Invalid → Error message to Telegram

📧 *Gmail sends full HTML with inline CSS — renders as a styled financial report*

💡 *The verdict (BUY/HOLD/AVOID) is extracted from the HTML and shown in the Telegram reply and email subject.*

## ❌ Error Handling

Two error paths are covered:

**Path A** — Invalid ticker (too short/long)
→ Caught before agent runs
→ Sends friendly Telegram hint

**Path B** — Agent failure or bad output
→ `continueOnFail` keeps workflow alive
→ IF node checks for valid HTML
→ Routes to this error message node

## ✉️ Email Recipient

Set your email in the **🔤 Extract Ticker & User** node — look for the `recipientEmail` field.

Change `REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_GMAIL@gmail.com` to your actual Gmail address.

*The Gmail account sending the report must be connected via OAuth2 in credentials.*

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
